description: Neuralingual user-facing MCP tool definitions. Single source of truth for tool names, descriptions, parameters, and handler routing.

tools.name: nl_library
tools.description: List all playlists in the user's library. Returns title, emoji, context, affirmation count, and render status for each playlist.

tools.name: nl_search
tools.description: Search playlists in the library by keyword. Matches against title, emoji, and session context.

query.description: Search keyword to match against title, emoji, or context

endpoint.note: Client-side filtering on the library response

tools.name: nl_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a playlist, including all affirmations, render config, and share status. Accepts full or short ID.

tools.name: nl_voices
tools.description: List available voices for rendering. Returns voice ID, name, gender, accent, and tier. Voices are used with nl_render_configure.

accent.description: Filter by accent (e.g. US, UK, AU)

endpoint.note: Public endpoint, no auth required. Client-side filtering.

tools.name: nl_create
tools.description: Create a new playlist. Provide intent text, source material (from a book, article, PDF, etc.), or both. When source is provided, AI generates affirmations grounded in that material. Costs 1 credit.

text.description: Intent text describing what you want to practice (e.g. "I am confident and capable"). Optional when sourceText is provided.

tone.description: Tone preference: grounded (default), open, or mystical

sourceText.description: Source material text (from a book, article, podcast transcript, etc.). When provided, affirmations are generated grounded in this material. Intent text becomes optional.

sourceTitle.description: Title of the source material (e.g. book title, article headline)

sourceUrl.description: URL of a web article. Server extracts the article text automatically. Mutually exclusive with sourceText and sourcePdf.

sourcePdf.description: Absolute file path to a PDF document. Server extracts text automatically. Max 10 MB, 20 pages. Mutually exclusive with sourceText, sourceUrl, and sourceYoutube.

sourceYoutube.description: YouTube video URL. Server extracts the transcript (captions) automatically. Mutually exclusive with sourceText, sourceUrl, and sourcePdf.

style.description: Named rhetorical-style preset to steer the writing style. Core: 'punchy' = very short, declarative, no filler (good when output feels too elaborate); 'plainspoken' = conversational and concrete; 'naval' = aphoristic and paradoxical. Persona pack (rhetorical cadence only — no quotes or impersonation): 'oprah' = warm, affirming, possibility-focused; 'dalai_lama' = serene, compassionate, spacious; 'marcus_aurelius' = stoic, terse maxims, control-vs-uncontrol; 'maya_angelou' = lyrical-but-grounded, cadenced, themes of rising; 'brene_brown' = warm, vulnerable, candid; 'rumi' = poetic, mystical, metaphor-rich (the most elaborate option); 'tony_robbins' = high-energy, decisive, action-framed (no hype). Combine with styleNotes to override individual dials.

styleNotes.description: Free-form rhetorical-style dials to fine-tune the writing style. Recognized keys: sentenceLength, ideaDensity, paradox, directness, register, structurePatterns (each a short phrase). When combined with 'style', these keys override the preset. Each value max 200 chars.

tools.name: nl_rename
tools.description: Update the title and/or emoji of a playlist. At least one of title or emoji must be provided.

tools.name: nl_sync_affirmations
tools.description: Declarative edit of affirmations in a playlist. Provide the complete desired list — affirmations not in the list are removed, new ones are added, existing ones are updated. Each affirmation needs text and enabled status. Include id for existing affirmations to preserve them.

tools.name: nl_delete
tools.description: Delete a playlist permanently. This cannot be undone.

tools.name: nl_render_configure
tools.description: Configure render settings for a playlist. Must be done before starting a render. Use nl_voices to find available voice IDs.

sessionContext.description: Session context for pacing and tone

backgroundAudioPath.description: Background sound storageKey (from nl_voices background list), or null to disable

playAll.description: Play all affirmations instead of fitting within duration

tools.name: nl_render_start
tools.description: Start rendering audio for a playlist. The set must have a render config (use nl_render_configure first). Returns a job ID for tracking progress.

tools.name: nl_render_status
tools.description: Check the render progress of a playlist. Returns status (none, queued, processing, completed, failed) and progress percentage.

tools.name: nl_rerender
tools.description: Re-render a playlist with its current config. Convenience wrapper that starts a new render job.

tools.name: nl_play
tools.description: Download rendered audio for a playlist and return the local file path. The audio must be fully rendered first (status: completed). The file is cached locally for future use.

endpoint.note: Requires library lookup to find the render job ID first

tools.name: nl_share
tools.description: Generate a public share link for a playlist. Anyone with the link can view and copy it.

tools.name: nl_unshare
tools.description: Revoke the share link for a practice set. The link will no longer work.

id.description: Practice set ID (full or short prefix)

tools.name: nl_credits
tools.description: Check the current credit balance. Shows subscription credits, purchased credits, and total available.

endpoint.note: Extracts credit fields from the user profile response

tools.name: nl_guide
tools.description: Render the framework (methodology, principles, sources, groupings, practical application, takeaway) for a playlist as markdown. Returns a clean fallback message for legacy sets that do not carry a framework — never errors on missing framework.

endpoint.note: Fetches intent detail, then renders framework client-side

tools.name: nl_set_export
tools.description: Export a playlist as a YAML string. Includes title, emoji, intent text, affirmations, and render config. The YAML can be edited and re-imported with nl_set_import. With withFramework=true, prepends the framework as markdown for human-facing contexts (the combined output is NOT re-importable via nl_set_import — apply expects pure YAML).

withFramework.description: When true, prepend the framework as markdown before the YAML body. Default false (pure YAML, re-importable). Legacy sets without a framework render a fallback notice instead of failing.

endpoint.note: Fetches intent detail, then serializes to YAML client-side

tools.name: nl_set_import
tools.description: Apply a YAML set file to an existing playlist. Updates title, emoji, affirmations, and render config based on the YAML content. Use nl_set_export to get the current YAML first, edit it, then import.

yaml.description: YAML content to apply (from nl_set_export, edited)

endpoint.note: Composite operation: PATCH intent + PUT affirmations/sync + PUT render-config

tools.name: nl_user_profile
tools.description: Get the authenticated user's profile including username, display name, email, subscription tier, credit balance, and role.

endpoint.note: Returns the full user profile with username

tools.name: nl_user_set_username
tools.description: Set or update the authenticated user's username. Usernames must be 3-30 characters, alphanumeric with underscores/hyphens. Returns the updated username and display name.

endpoint.note: Sets username field via the profile update endpoint

tools.name: nl_user_check_username
tools.description: Check if a username is available. Returns availability status and a suggested alternative if the username is taken.

endpoint.note: Public endpoint but routed through authenticated client for base URL resolution

tools.name: nl_catalog_browse
tools.description: Browse the curated catalog of playlists. Supports filtering by category, session context, and sorting. Returns metadata for each catalog item including title, description, category, audio availability, and duration.

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return (default: 20)

offset.description: Number of items to skip for pagination (default: 0)

endpoint.note: Public endpoint. Category filtering and pagination are client-side; context/sort/filter are server-side query params.

tools.name: nl_catalog_view
tools.description: View a specific catalog item by slug. Returns full details including affirmations, framework, inspirations, voice info, and render configuration.

endpoint.note: Public endpoint, no auth required.

tools.name: nl_catalog_copy
tools.description: Copy a catalog playlist to the user's library. Creates a full copy including affirmations, render config, and pre-rendered audio. The new playlist appears in nl_library_list immediately.

slug.description: Catalog item slug (from nl_catalog_browse or nl_catalog_view)

endpoint.note: Requires authentication. Rate-limited (same as intent creation).

tools.name: nl_library_list
tools.description: List all playlists (playlists) in the user's library with metadata including title, emoji, context, affirmation count, render status, play count, and last played date.

tools.name: nl_library_view
tools.description: View a specific playlist with its affirmations, render status, and play statistics. Shows each affirmation's text, enabled status, and feedback (liked/disliked). Accepts full or short ID.

endpoint.note: Fetches intent detail and formats as playlist view

tools.name: nl_affirmations_feedback
tools.description: Set feedback (like or dislike) on one or more affirmations in a playlist. Supports batch: provide multiple affirmation IDs. Uses continue-on-error — partial failures are reported per-affirmation. Use null feedback to clear.

affirmationIds.description: Affirmation IDs to set feedback on (use nl_library_view to find IDs)

feedback.description: Feedback value: 'liked', 'disliked', or null to clear

endpoint.note: Iterates over individual affirmation feedback endpoints

tools.name: nl_affirmations_toggle
tools.description: Enable or disable one or more affirmations in a playlist. Supports batch: provide multiple affirmation IDs. All toggles are applied atomically in a single transaction.

affirmationIds.description: Affirmation IDs to toggle (use nl_library_view to find IDs)

isEnabled.description: true to enable, false to disable

endpoint.note: Resolves intent to set, then calls batch-toggle endpoint

tools.name: nl_context_settings_list
tools.description: List all per-context default settings (voice, duration, binaural, pace, background, etc.). Returns customized contexts only — contexts not listed use system defaults.

tools.name: nl_context_settings_update
tools.description: Update default settings for a specific session context. Set individual fields to override system defaults. Set a field to null to clear that override.

context.description: Session context to update defaults for

voiceId.description: Default voice ID for this context (null to clear)

durationMinutes.description: Default duration in minutes (null to clear)

binauralPreset.description: Default binaural beat preset (null to disable)

paceWpm.description: Default pace in words per minute (null to clear)

backgroundKey.description: Default background sound key (null to disable)

subliminalEnabled.description: Whether subliminal layer is enabled by default (null to clear)

repeatCount.description: Default repeat count per affirmation (null to clear)

pauseMs.description: Default pause between affirmations in ms (null to clear)

tools.name: nl_context_settings_reset
tools.description: Reset a context's settings to system defaults. Removes all custom overrides for the specified context.

tools.name: nl_wizard_defaults
tools.description: Get the resolved wizard defaults (voice, background, binaural, duration, playback mode). Returns the effective defaults based on a 4-level cascade: intent-specific config > recent config > onboarding preferences > system defaults.

intentId.description: Optional intent ID to resolve intent-specific defaults from

tools.name: nl_source_extract
tools.description: Extract text preview from a URL (article, blog post, etc.). Returns title, author, character count, and excerpt. Useful for previewing source material before feeding it into nl_create.

url.description: URL of the web page to extract text from

tools.name: nl_source_youtube
tools.description: Extract transcript from a YouTube video URL. Returns title, channel name, character count, and video ID. The transcript can then be used as source material with nl_create.

tools.name: nl_source_twitter
tools.description: Extract thread text from a Twitter/X post URL. Returns the post text, author name, handle, and character count. The text can then be used as source material with nl_create.

tools.name: nl_source_pdf
tools.description: Upload a local PDF file and extract its text content. Returns the extracted text, page count, and character count. The text can then be used as source material with nl_create. Maximum file size is 10 MB.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the PDF file on the local filesystem

endpoint.note: Reads local file, uploads as multipart form data

tools.name: nl_playback_start
tools.description: Log the start of a practice session (playback). Creates a playback history record for the given playlist. The playlist must exist in the user's library. Optionally accepts a render job ID for analytics. Returns the playback record ID for use with nl_playback_complete.

renderJobId.description: Render job ID for analytics tracking (optional)

tools.name: nl_playback_complete
tools.description: Update a playback session with completion status. Records the duration in seconds and optionally marks the session as completed. Use the playback ID returned by nl_playback_start.

durationSeconds.description: Duration of the playback session in seconds

completed.description: Whether the session was fully completed (default: false)

tools.name: nl_generate_more
tools.description: Generate additional affirmations for an existing playlist using AI. Adds new affirmations alongside the existing ones. Costs 1 credit. The count parameter is only honored for free regeneration (replacing removed affirmations); paid generation uses the server default batch size.

count.description: Number of affirmations to generate (only honored for free regeneration; paid uses server default)

endpoint.note: Resolves intent to its latest affirmation set, then generates more affirmations

tools.name: nl_affirmation_add
tools.description: Add a custom user-written affirmation to a playlist. The affirmation text must be 1-240 characters.

endpoint.note: Resolves intent to its latest affirmation set, then adds the affirmation

tools.name: nl_affirmation_delete
tools.description: Delete a single affirmation from a playlist. Cannot delete if it would leave fewer than 5 non-disliked enabled affirmations.

affirmationId.description: ID of the affirmation to delete (use nl_library_view to find IDs)

endpoint.note: Validates affirmation belongs to the intent's latest set before deleting

tools.name: nl_intent_delete
tools.description: Delete a playlist (intent) permanently. This cannot be undone. Alias for nl_delete with consistent naming.

tools.name: nl_intent_update
tools.description: Update a playlist's intent text, title, emoji, and/or tone preference. At least one field must be provided.

text.description: New intent text (what you want to practice)
